mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the preceding 3 months signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.42% on 2019-04-01 reflects recent Fed rate cuts
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reduce exposure.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% on 2019-04-01 reflects tight labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market stress signals
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or correlation between UNRATE changes and 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 513356M and HOUST at 1139K on 2019-03-01 show demand sector values
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above its 12-month trend, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization that supports corporate profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 109.1818 on 2019-04-01 reflects industrial activity level
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with supply acceleration
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances where INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month, or Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100,000 jobs per month, signaling labor market weakness that precedes broader economic slowdown and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors anticipate earnings compression.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151095K on 2019-04-01 shows employment level
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market deceleration
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where monthly PAYEMS growth falls below 100,000, or the correlation between PAYEMS growth and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates below its 12-month trend while the federal funds rate remains elevated above 2.0%, it signals monetary policy tightness relative to inflation dynamics, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as real rates compress equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 254.958 and FEDFUNDS at 2.42% on 2019-04-01
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between CPIAUCSL deceleration and elevated FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized rate, signaling economic slowdown that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18912.326B on 2019-01-01 reflects real economic output
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances where GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0%, or the correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1,200 thousand units annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) remain stable or growing, signaling robust consumer demand and housing investment that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1200K units AND RSAFS month-over-month growth >= 0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1139K and RSAFS at 513356M on 2019-03-01
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with strong consumer demand
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint interaction effect of HOUST acceleration and RSAFS stability is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.
